content.text: is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's

engine. It lets you run JavaScript outside the browser — on servers, build tools, CLIs, and desktop applications. For Angular developers, Node.js is the environment that runs the Angular CLI, the TypeScript compiler, webpack/esbuild, and any server-side rendering (SSR) setup.
for JavaScript execution but handles concurrent I/O through an
backed by libuv. This makes it efficient for I/O-bound tasks without the complexity of multi-threading.

The event loop is the heart of Node.js. It continuously checks for work to do, processes it, and repeats. This explains why Node.js can handle thousands of concurrent connections with a single thread.
setTimeout / setInterval callbacks whose delay has elapsed
I/O callbacks deferred from previous iteration
Retrieve new I/O events; execute callbacks
) run between each phase — they drain completely before the next phase begins.
Node.js uses a release cycle where
become Long-Term Support (LTS) releases, maintained for 30 months. Angular CLI requires a minimum Node.js version — check the Angular compatibility table before upgrading.

# Check current Node.js version
node --version      # e.g. v20.11.0
node -e "console.log(process.versions)"

# Check npm version
npm --version
(Node Version Manager) lets you install and switch between multiple Node.js versions on the same machine. This is essential when working on projects with different Angular / Node.js requirements.

# Install nvm (macOS / Linux)
curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash

# Install a specific Node.js version
nvm install 20          # installs latest Node 20.x
nvm install --lts       # installs latest LTS

# Switch versions
nvm use 20

# Set default
nvm alias default 20

# List installed versions
nvm ls

# .nvmrc — pin project to a version (committed to git)
echo "20" > .nvmrc
nvm use   # reads .nvmrc automatically
nvm is not available on Windows. Use
(github.com/coreybutler/nvm-windows) or Volta, which works cross-platform and automatically switches Node versions per project.
Node.js supports two module formats: the legacy
(.mjs or "type": "module" in package.json, using import / export). TypeScript compiles to CommonJS by default for Node scripts but to ESM for Angular browser builds.
When Node.js resolves a bare specifier like
, it searches: built-in modules first, then
in the current directory and all parent directories up to the filesystem root.

# Run a script
node app.js
node dist/main.js

# Execute a one-liner
node -e "console.log(process.platform)"

# REPL (interactive shell)
node
> 1 + 1

# Watch mode (Node 18+) — restarts on file changes
node --watch app.js

# TypeScript via ts-node or tsx (not built-in)
npx ts-node script.ts
npx tsx script.ts    # faster, uses esbuild

Node.js provides globals not available in browsers, and is missing some browser globals. Angular applications run in the browser, but SSR and build tools run in Node.js — guard platform-specific code accordingly.
Every Angular development command runs inside Node.js. Understanding what happens under the hood helps diagnose slow builds and out-of-memory errors.
Starts esbuild dev server; watches files; compiles TypeScript
Runs esbuild/webpack bundle pipeline; outputs to dist/
Starts Karma test runner via Node
Reads/writes files using Node fs API
Installs npm packages; runs Angular schematics
, your Angular application runs in Node.js on the server. APIs unavailable in Node.js (
Tracing execution order helps explain Node.js performance. The example below shows when each async mechanism resolves.
runs before any other microtask, including Promises. Use it sparingly: a recursive nextTick loop starves the event loop.
is preferred when you want to defer to the check phase after I/O.
Because JavaScript runs on a single thread, CPU-intensive synchronous operations
— no other callbacks can run while they execute. Servers handling concurrent requests must offload heavy computation to worker threads.
object is a global in Node.js (no require needed). It provides process information, environment access, and lifecycle hooks. It is heavily used in Angular build scripts, SSR servers, and CLI tools.
Streams handle large amounts of data without loading it all into memory. The
module, HTTP requests/responses, and process stdio are all streams.
In Angular SSR, the Angular renderer can stream HTML to the client incrementally using
with a streaming response, reducing Time to First Byte (TTFB).
Node.js has built-in debugging via the V8 inspector protocol, compatible with Chrome DevTools and VS Code.

# Attach inspector
node --inspect app.js          # listens on localhost:9229
node --inspect-brk app.js     # breaks before first line

# Chrome: open chrome://inspect and click 'inspect'

# VS Code .vscode/launch.json
{
  "configurations": [
    {
      "type": "node",
      "request": "launch",
      "name": "Debug script",
      "program": "${workspaceFolder}/dist/server.js",
      "sourceMaps": true,
      "outFiles": ["${workspaceFolder}/dist/**/*.js"]
    }
  ]
}

# Angular SSR debugging
node --inspect dist/my-app/server/server.mjs

# Memory usage snapshot
node --expose-gc -e "global.gc(); console.log(process.memoryUsage())"

# CPU profiling
node --prof app.js            # writes isolate-*.log
node --prof-process isolate-*.log > profile.txt
Not available in Node.js -- throws ReferenceError
Guard with isPlatformBrowser() or inject DOCUMENT token
Synchronous file I/O in request handlers
readFileSync blocks the event loop for all requests
Use fs.promises.readFile or the async callback form
Explicitly add .js extension to all relative imports
Node exits with code 1 in Node 15+

module provides both synchronous and asynchronous APIs for reading, writing, and manipulating files. Angular CLI and build tools rely heavily on fs — understanding it helps when writing custom schematics, build scripts, or server-side rendering logic.
instead of string concatenation — they handle OS-specific separators automatically.
. Angular projects use a combination of Node-level env vars (for the build/CLI) and Angular environment files (src/environments/) for runtime config.
at startup — commonly used in Angular SSR servers and custom Node scripts.
module that can create a server without any framework. Angular Universal (SSR) and the Angular CLI dev-server both build on similar concepts.
When Angular Universal renders pages server-side, it runs inside a Node.js HTTP server. The
package wraps this same http module with a router, middleware pipeline, and sensible defaults — but every
call ultimately creates a native Node.js server.
is a JavaScript runtime built on V8 that powers the Angular CLI, TypeScript compiler, and Angular SSR.
The event loop enables high-concurrency I/O without multi-threading — callbacks run when I/O completes, not synchronously.
(or nvm-windows / Volta) manages multiple versions per machine; commit

to avoid OOM errors during large Angular builds.
